description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of sport psychology: motivation, goal-setting, concentration, stress management, and teamwork.

questions.text: What is the main area of sport psychology?
questions.explanation: Sport psychology deals with the mental and psychological factors that influence sports performance: motivation, concentration, stress management, and team dynamics.

options.text: The mental and psychological factors that influence sports performance

questions.explanation: True. Both intrinsic motivation (e.g., desire for improvement) and extrinsic motivation (e.g., reward, recognition) determine the level and quality of training effort.

questions.text: What is the role of goal-setting in sport?
questions.explanation: Specific, measurable, and achievable goals give training direction, increase motivation, and enable tracking of progress.

options.text: Guaranteeing the outcome of the competition
Giving direction, increasing motivation, and tracking progress

questions.text: What is the role of concentration in sports activity?
questions.explanation: Concentration is the deliberate direction of attention to relevant stimuli: during a match, tracking the ball, teammates, and opponents' movements is the foundation of effective decision-making.

options.text: Directing attention to relevant stimuli for effective decision-making

questions.text: Pre-competition nerves always negatively affect performance.
questions.explanation: False. Moderate stress (arousal) stimulates performance — the optimal level of arousal improves focus and reaction readiness. Only excessive stress reduces performance.
Intrinsic motivation stems from genuine interest in and enjoyment of the activity itself (e.g., the pleasure of improving), and is not dependent on external rewards.

options.text: Genuine internal interest in and enjoyment of the activity

questions.text: The psychological factors of teamwork influence team performance.
questions.explanation: True. Team cohesion, trust, communication, and shared goals are all psychological factors that strongly determine the collective performance of the team.

questions.text: What is the goal of mental preparation in sport?
questions.explanation: The goal of mental preparation is for the athlete to perform optimally even in competitive situations: to be able to handle pressure, maintain focus, and respond with mental flexibility.

options.text: To achieve optimal performance even under pressure

questions.text: Which is one effective stress management technique in sport?
questions.explanation: Deep breathing (relaxation techniques) reduces the stress response of the autonomic nervous system, helping the athlete to calm down before competition or in high-pressure situations.

questions.text: Sport psychology preparation is only necessary for professional athletes.
questions.explanation: False. Developing mental skills (motivation, concentration, stress management) is beneficial at every level — for amateur and youth athletes just as much as for professionals.
